summary: This brief post shares two Microsoft reference resources: a comparative list of programming language equivalents across VB, J#, C++, C#, JScript, and Visual FoxPro, and a Win32 to .NET API mapping guide. Atwood positions these as practical tools for developers working across Microsoft's language ecosystem. The post includes a tongue-in-cheek reference to the debate about whether .NET is truly a 'thin wrapper' over Win32.
key_points: Microsoft provided official documentation comparing syntax across their supported languages
The Win32 to .NET API map was a useful resource for developers transitioning from native to managed code
The 'thin wrapper' characterization of .NET over Win32 was a common but debated simplification
money_quotes: Since .NET is just a thin wrapper over Win32 (or so I've been told)
key_insight: Cross-language reference materials were essential tools for developers navigating Microsoft's diverse language ecosystem in the early .NET era.
